verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a type I single-pass TM protein predominantly resident in the ER membrane, where it senses ER Ca2+ depletion and clusters at ER-PM junctions to activate ORAI1/TRPC channels (inner-leaflet apposition, not surface exposure). However, STIM1 was originally identified as a PM protein, and a minority fraction (~20-30% by surface biotinylation) is documented on the plasma membrane in multiple cell types (VSMCs, pancreatic epithelial cells), with the N-terminal EF-hand/SAM ectodomain accessible to extracellular antibodies that inhibit Ca2+ entry. Recent work confirms dual topology at the PM. This is a documented dual-localization with its own TM anchor at the surface.
